steps.title: Identify the context clue
steps.content: The strongest signal is that Alexander is "very busy," has been at work almost constantly, and avoids social activities — painting a picture of uninterrupted work activity.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The word "constantly" needs to mean without stopping — Alexander avoided clubs and worked almost ______ every day. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Enough: he was at work almost enough — enough means a sufficient amount; it describes a quantity, not the continuous nature of his effort. ✗
  • Choice B — Nonstop: he was at work almost nonstop — nonstop means without interruption or pause, matching the picture of someone who avoids all social activity to keep working. ✓
  • Choice C — Quickly: he was at work almost quickly — quickly describes speed, not duration or continuity; the passage says nothing about how fast he worked. ✗
  • Choice D — Cautiously: he was at work almost cautiously — cautiously means with care or wariness; the passage emphasizes the unbroken nature of his work, not his carefulness. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
